name: Michigan
tagline: A historic AI data-center boom anchored by Stargate, fresh sales-tax incentives, and a fully centralized DTMB make Michigan one of the strongest AI-infrastructure markets in the Midwest.

execSummary.summary: Michigan is in transition heading into 2026, and three things matter most for an SLG seller. First, technology is almost entirely centralized in one department — the Department of Technology, Management & Budget (DTMB) — which owns IT, procurement, and budget, so the buying motion is unusually consolidated. Second, Michigan has become a national AI-infrastructure destination: the OpenAI/Oracle/Related Digital 'Stargate' campus in Saline Township (~1.4GW, called the largest one-time investment in state history) plus new enterprise data-center sales-tax exemptions extended to 2050/2065 are pulling hyperscale and enterprise compute into the state. Third, leadership is in flux — DTMB has an acting director and acting CIO as of early 2026, and a new state Chief Security Officer arrived in December 2025 — so relationships and account mapping are being reset. Dell's clearest plays are AI-infrastructure and research-computing demand, endpoint and lifecycle refresh across a centralized estate, and governed AI as agencies operationalize DTMB's 2025 AI guidelines.
opportunities: AI-infrastructure and 'AI factory' demand tied to the Stargate buildout and broader data-center boom (PowerEdge, networking, services).
Centralized DTMB estate refresh — endpoint, client, server, and lifecycle across consolidated state IT.
Governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive workloads as agencies stand up DTMB's AI guidelines and any generative-AI pilot.
University research computing (University of Michigan, Michigan State) as independent large-scale AI-infrastructure buyers.

budget.intro: Michigan budgets on an annual fiscal cycle ending September 30. Governor Whitmer's eighth and final executive recommendation, presented February 11, 2026, proposes roughly $88.1B for FY2027, against a projected ~$1.8B gap driven by softening revenue, rising health costs, and federal fiscal impacts. There is no single 'state IT budget' line; technology spend is centralized in DTMB and distributed across agency services, with a dedicated Information Technology Investment Fund for modernization.
notes: Figures are annual (single fiscal year) unless noted; FY2027 numbers are an executive proposal, not enacted.
Technology spend is centralized in DTMB and largely embedded in agency service charges rather than broken out as a single IT line.

FY2027 budget not yet enacted as of June 2026: the Senate passed ~$88.1B (Apr 29) but the GOP-led House and the Dem Senate/Governor are split, with a continuation/stopgap budget floated that could delay a deal past the November elections; a second straight missed July 1 deadline is a live risk. (Governor's proposal, per NASBO: $13.6B GF, -3.6% vs FY2026; $88.1B all funds; $400M rainy-day withdrawal leaving reserves near an all-time high.)

initiatives.name: Stargate & AI Data-Center Boom
initiatives.summary: The OpenAI/Oracle/Related Digital 'Stargate' campus in Saline Township (~1.4GW power, multi-billion-dollar, called the largest one-time investment in state history) advanced to active construction in mid-2026 — the June 1 'The Barn' milestone marked three ~550,000 sq ft halls (~1.65M sq ft total), completion targeted end-2027, despite local opposition. It anchors a wider data-center wave drawn by Michigan's expanded sales/use-tax exemptions. This is the strongest tailwind for Dell AI-infrastructure and ISG demand in the state.

initiatives.name: Enterprise Data-Center Tax Exemption
initiatives.summary: Public Act 207 of 2024 (signed Jan 2025) extends the data-center equipment sales/use-tax exemption through 2050 for qualified centers and creates a new enterprise exemption through 2065 for brownfield/former-powerplant sites. Qualification requires $250M+ investment and 30+ jobs at 150% of regional median wage — structurally favoring large, hardware-intensive buildouts.

initiatives.name: DTMB AI Guidelines & Generative-AI Pilot
initiatives.summary: DTMB issued statewide AI use guidelines in 2025, and HB 5899 (introduced 2026) would create an AI governing board and a controlled generative-AI pilot across agencies with mandatory human review of outputs. Agencies need governed, auditable AI — an opening for private/on-prem AI on sensitive data rather than open public models.

initiatives.name: MIHI Broadband Expansion (BEAD)
initiatives.summary: The Michigan High-Speed Internet Office (MIHI) secured a $920M BEAD award — third-largest pool of eligible locations in the nation — for ~$1.47B total with private match, connecting ~200,000 locations over 31,000+ miles of fiber. Construction begins 2026. Indirect Dell value: edge, networking, and the connected public-sector endpoints that follow.

initiatives.name: Statewide Cybersecurity Modernization
initiatives.summary: DTMB runs cybersecurity centrally for the enterprise; a new state Chief Security Officer (Rex Menold) stepped in December 2025, and the annual Michigan Cyber Summit continues to anchor state/local partnerships. Leadership turnover plus a tight budget mean security spend is being re-prioritized — a window to lead with secure compute, infrastructure, and resilience services.

procurement.intro: DTMB is the central technology procurement authority and consolidates IT, procurement, facilities, and budget under one executive department — a structure cited as a national best practice. State technology buys run through DTMB-managed statewide contracts, and the MiDEAL extended purchasing program lets local governments, K-12, universities, community colleges, tribes, and nonprofit hospitals buy off those same contracts without running their own RFP.

vehicles.name: MiDEAL Extended Purchasing Program
vehicles.detail: Primary cooperative vehicle (since 1984; 1,300+ members). Lets local gov, schools, universities, and nonprofit hospitals buy from state contracts. Confirm Dell agreements and resellers are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: DTMB Statewide Contracts (Contract Connect)
vehicles.detail: Pre-competed master agreements for state agencies across IT hardware, software, and services — the backbone of the centralized buy.

vehicles.name: SUITE / centralized IT services
vehicles.detail: State Unified Information Technology Environment standards; DTMB delivers consolidated IT as a shared service to agencies.

entities.name: DTMB
entities.role: Central IT, procurement, facilities, and budget authority — owns the statewide technology motion.

entities.name: Dept of Health & Human Services (MDHHS)
entities.role: Among the largest agency IT consumers; data-heavy modernization.

entities.name: University of Michigan
entities.role: Independent large-scale research-computing and AI-infrastructure buyer.

entities.name: Michigan State University
entities.role: Independent research-computing buyer; separate procurement from the state.

procurement.competition: Michigan is a centralized, contract-driven market — incumbents hold DTMB statewide agreements across compute, networking, and services. Differentiation comes from AI-infrastructure capability tied to the data-center boom, governed-AI and security offerings, and MiDEAL-ready packaging for local/education buyers rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Gretchen Whitmer
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: In her final year (term ends Jan 2027); champions the 'Make It in Michigan' agenda and the Stargate/data-center wins.

powerStructure.name: Kyle Guerrant
powerStructure.title: Acting Director, DTMB
powerStructure.note: Appointed acting director January 2026; oversees centralized IT, procurement, and budget.

powerStructure.name: Eric Swanson
powerStructure.title: Acting State CIO
powerStructure.note: Acting CIO directing state technology and security; longtime DTMB leader who built the Center for Shared Solutions.

powerStructure.name: Rex Menold
powerStructure.title: Chief Security Officer
powerStructure.note: Stepped into the state CSO role December 2025; owns enterprise cybersecurity direction.

news.title: Stargate 'The Barn' construction celebrated in Saline Township
news.impact: Oracle/OpenAI/Related Digital and Gov. Whitmer mark construction of a ~1GW (1.4GW power) campus — three ~550,000 sq ft halls, ~1.65M sq ft total, completion targeted end-2027. Confirms Michigan as a top-tier AI-infrastructure market despite local opposition.

news.title: FY2027 budget unsigned; stopgap risk before July 1 deadline
news.impact: Senate passed ~$88.1B (Apr 29) but the GOP-led House and Dem Senate/Governor remain split; House Speaker floated a continuation budget that could push a deal past the Nov elections. A second straight missed July 1 deadline would delay FY2027 state IT procurement decisions.

news.title: DTMB gets acting director (Kyle Guerrant) and acting CIO (Eric Swanson)
news.impact: Leadership reset at the central IT authority; both seats still acting as of June 2026 — account mapping and relationships in flux.

news.title: Michigan celebrates $1.47B high-speed internet investment ($920M BEAD)
news.impact: Large fiber buildout begins 2026; downstream edge, networking, and endpoint demand.

news.title: Rex Menold named State Chief Security Officer
news.impact: New owner of enterprise cybersecurity strategy — a fresh relationship to build.

news.title: Whitmer announces Saline Township data center as 'largest investment in state history'
news.impact: Signals the scale of AI-infrastructure capital landing in Michigan.

dellActions: Lead with AI infrastructure: tie Dell ISG / AI Factory offerings to the Stargate buildout, the data-center tax incentives, and university research-computing buyers.
Re-map DTMB now — engage the acting director, acting CIO, and new CSO early while relationships are being reset under a centralized authority.
Validate Dell's DTMB statewide-contract and MiDEAL coverage and resellers are current and correctly scoped for both state and local/education buyers.
Position governed, private/on-prem AI for sensitive workloads as agencies operationalize DTMB's AI guidelines and any generative-AI pilot.
Frame modernization around ROI given the ~$1.8B budget gap — endpoint/lifecycle refresh and resilience services that pay back quickly.
